message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>We propose a linear model of infection probability, and prove that this is a good approximation to a more refined model in which we assume infections come from a series of independent risks. We argue that the linearity assumption makes interpreting and using the model much easier, without significantly diminishing the reliability of the model.</jats:p>
title: Policy Implications of an Approximate Linear Infection Model for SARS-CoV-2
